Transcrição Completa: 53 What Is A VAK

 

[00:00]You might have heard me mention a few times this threeletter acronym called VAK, which stands for visual auditory kinesthetic.

 

[00:09]With the VAK, it comes from a book called Frogs in a Princes, which is an introduction to neural linguistic programming by a guy named Richard Bandler.

 

[00:19]Richard Bandler is a great guy to study. I'd recommend getting all of his books.

 

[00:24]But in addition to that, what I pulled from it was one of the most effective ways to communicate ever, the VAK, visual auditory kinesthetic.

 

[00:33]Everybody has one medium for how they interpret information and how they output information.

 

[00:40]Sometimes people have two or they're a combination or in other times they have a one way that they interpret data and they have a completely different way that they will teach other people information or express their ideas to others.

 

[00:54]But they'll always do it in these three buckets. Visual, auditory or kinesthetic.

 

[01:00]So let me break down what each one is. Visual people will use words with you or they will prefer to interpret data with visual information meaning visual interpretation.

 

[01:13]Visuals that explain the processes to things with little wheels and icons and arrows pointing which way. Right?

 

[01:20]A visual person will prefer when you're explaining a process to for you to use websites like Funnelytics which will help you with demonstrating the traffic sources and all the steps and all the emails that are a part of the process.

 

[01:35]There's so many cool ways that you can visually present data to people.

 

[01:39]A visual person, they'll identify themselves with you in conversation by saying things like, "Can you show me what you're talking about?"

 

[01:47]Or, "Can we get a better picture of what we're going to do together to make some money?"

 

[01:52]"Can you do me a favor and send it over? I want to take a look at it."

 

[01:57]You can always identify visual people with their words, of course, that they use and with ways that they express themselves.

 

[02:04]Visual people are usually matched better. You know, they're usually visually well put together.

 

[02:10]They might have whiter teeth than somebody who's an auditory person comparatively.

 

[02:15]You know, a visual person might wear an uncomfortable pair of shoes because they look better than the comfortable pair would have.

 

[02:23]Visual people are easy to identify in many different ways. But make sure when you approach that person, you recognize that they prefer information interpreted and they'll output information to you in a visual way.

 

[02:36]Okay. The A of the VAK, auditory. So auditory people, they obviously prefer information being explained to them and articulated to them with words.

 

[02:48]They'd prefer to learn with things like podcasts or audio books rather than reading a physical book and potentially like a kinesthetic person would feeling the book.

 

[02:59]Rather than a visual person would just prefer to look at it. Maybe not maybe wouldn't prefer the book. They could read an ebook rather than the kinesthetic person would prefer the touch.

 

[03:11]Long story short, auditory people, they'll identify themselves in simple ways. They'll say things like, "Hey, can you explain that to me? I'd love to talk about it.

 

[03:21]When when can we get on the phone so we can go over the deal together?"

 

[03:26]They're going to retract from things like getting on a Zoom call. If they do get on a Zoom conversation or Skype conversation with you, they're they're probably not going to use the video.

 

[03:37]Auditory people, they tend, not always, but they tend to not be as worried about matching.

 

[03:43]They'll wear like a like a green and black shirt and like I don't know blue or red shorts or something like that and maybe a gray shoes or something, you know, or like anything. It just be a mismatch kind of outfit.

 

[03:56]They wouldn't be as focused on the visual outcome of how they're presented. You know, their hair might be a little messed up.

 

[04:03]Maybe they're just once again not as well put together, not as visually presented as what a V would be comparatively.

 

[04:12]And now an auditory person, you know, just between those two of of the V and the A, once again, they're going to prefer you to explain things in long drawn out processes because that's how they're going to interpret data.

 

[04:26]You might be a visual person yourself and how you output information. But that's why I'm going over the VAK. It's adaptability to increase your effectiveness of communication.

 

[04:38]So, it doesn't matter what you are. It matters how you pivot when you identify what the other person is for how you can best get your ideas and what you're articulating into the other person's head.

 

[04:51]The K, okay, kinesthetic. These people operate off actions or they operate off of emotions.

 

[04:58]A kinesthetic person, they use their senses to make decisions. They'll they'll operate off of gut feelings or emotional states that they get put into.

 

[05:09]So kinesthetic people, they actually play the majority in total between the VAK. 35% of people are visual, 25% of people are auditory, and 40% are kinesthetic.

 

[05:24]So the odds are in your favor. If you're wondering what somebody is without having a way to interpret them clearly, go with the K or go with a V because those are the two highest probabilities.

 

[05:36]K being 40% and the V being 35%.

 

[05:40]So, long story short, kinesthetic people, they're going to prefer things like, can you break down the process for me? Can you demonstrate how this would work? I want to know how we get from A to B here.

 

[05:53]Notice that those are terms that use verbs. They'd say things to you like, yeah, you know, I was I was going over this, like a verb. I was going over this.

 

[06:04]I loved the video an emotion and I'd love to move forward on talking with you about it. That's a kinesthetic individual.

 

[06:13]You see all those verbs, all those action terms. They also might recognize emotional states to you more than what other people will.

 

[06:21]They might say things like, "Yeah, I was feeling great, so I had taken this particular action because I was just I was in the zone."

 

[06:29]Kinesthetic people will express things to you and will represent themselves. They're easier to spot. You know, they kind of wear their heart on their sleeve is a is a misinterpreted way to say it.

 

[06:41]Meaning they'll just have more emotional states readily demonstrated to the world.

 

[06:46]You know, their videos might be a little more amplified or passionate oriented or once again with the words you can identify clearly.

 

[06:55]So, in sales, your ability to adapt to the person you're speaking to is going to increase your ability to get your points across and to make more money and increases your chances of closing more deals.

 

[07:08]Okay, I'll see you in the next video.

 

 

📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO

 

NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:

35% das pessoas são Visuais (V)

25% das pessoas são Auditivas (A)

40% das pessoas são Kinestésicas (K)

K e V juntos representam 75% das pessoas (maiores probabilidades)

 

ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:

Sistema VAK: Framework de comunicação baseado em Programação Neurolinguística

Estratégia de Adaptação: Identificar o tipo VAK do prospect e adaptar comunicação

Identificação por Palavras-Chave:

Visual: "mostrar", "ver", "olhar", "picture"

Auditória: "explicar", "falar", "conversar", "ouvir"

Kinestésica: verbos de ação, "sentir", "fazer", "mover"

Ferramentas Visuais: Uso de Funnelytics para apresentar processos com ícones, setas, fluxos

Método de Apresentação Adaptativa: Ajustar formato (visual, verbal, demonstração) conforme o tipo VAK

 

ESTRUTURA:

Framework VAK (Visual-Auditory-Kinesthetic):

Origem: Livro "Frogs into Princes" por Richard Bandler

Base: Programação Neurolinguística (PNL)

Três buckets de processamento de informação

Perfil Visual (V - 35%):

Preferência: Diagramas, gráficos, ícones, setas

Características físicas: Bem vestidos, matching de cores, dentes brancos

Palavras-chave: "show", "picture", "look"

Comportamento: Sacrificam conforto por aparência

Perfil Auditório (A - 25%):

Preferência: Podcasts, audiobooks, conversas telefônicas

Características físicas: Menos preocupados com matching, cabelo bagunçado

Palavras-chave: "explain", "talk", "hear"

Comportamento: Evitam vídeo calls, preferem áudio

Perfil Kinestésico (K - 40%):

Preferência: Ação, demonstração, sensações físicas

Características emocionais: Demonstram emoções abertamente

Palavras-chave: Verbos de ação, "feel", "move", "do"

Comportamento: Decisões baseadas em gut feeling e estados emocionais

 

DADOS FINANCEIROS:

Não especificados diretamente, mas mencionado:

Aumento na taxa de fechamento de vendas com adaptação VAK

Maior capacidade de fazer dinheiro através de comunicação efetiva

Melhora na conversão de deals ao identificar e adaptar ao tipo VAK do prospect

 

APLICAÇÕES PRÁTICAS:

Em Vendas: Adaptar pitch conforme tipo VAK identificado

Em Apresentações: Usar recursos visuais para V, explicações verbais para A, demonstrações práticas para K

Em Follow-ups: Enviar materiais no formato preferido (infográficos para V, áudio para A, vídeos de demonstração para K)

Probabilidade de Acerto: Começar assumindo K (40%) ou V (35%) quando em dúvida

 

AUTOR REFERENCIADO:

Richard Bandler - Criador da Programação Neurolinguística

Livro: "Frogs into Princes"

Recomendação: Estudar todos os livros do autor

 

DURAÇÃO: 07:10 FORMATO: Vídeo educacional / Aula de programa de vendas e comunicação
